name: Ex Works
summary: Seller makes goods available at their premises. Buyer bears all costs and risks from that point.
seller_responsibility: Make goods available at named place (usually seller's premises). Export packaging.
buyer_responsibility: All transport, insurance, export/import clearance, duties, and risk from seller's premises to destination.
risk_transfer: At seller's premises when goods are placed at buyer's disposal.
export_clearance: Buyer (though seller must assist if requested).
best_for: Domestic transactions or when buyer has strong logistics capability. Least obligation for seller.
watch_out: Buyer assumes risk at seller's door — if goods are damaged during loading, buyer bears the loss. Not recommended for international trade as buyer must handle export clearance in seller's country.

name: Free Carrier
summary: Seller delivers goods to carrier or named place. Risk transfers when goods are handed to the first carrier.
seller_responsibility: Deliver goods to carrier at named place. Export clearance. Loading onto collecting vehicle if delivery is at seller's premises.
buyer_responsibility: Main carriage, insurance, import clearance, duties, unloading at destination.
risk_transfer: When goods are delivered to the carrier at the named place.
cost_transfer: At the named place of delivery to carrier.
best_for: International trade where seller wants to handle export but not main carriage. Most versatile INCOTERM for multimodal transport.
watch_out: New in 2020: FCA can now include a provision for the buyer to instruct their carrier to issue a bill of lading to the seller (useful for letter of credit transactions).

name: Carriage Paid To
summary: Seller pays freight to named destination. Risk transfers when goods are handed to the first carrier.
seller_responsibility: Main carriage costs to named destination. Export clearance. Delivery to first carrier.
buyer_responsibility: Insurance (optional), import clearance, duties, unloading at destination.
risk_transfer: When goods are delivered to the first carrier (NOT at destination).
cost_transfer: At named destination (freight paid by seller).
best_for: When seller arranges and pays for transport but doesn't want to bear risk during transit.
watch_out: Risk and cost transfer at DIFFERENT points — risk transfers at first carrier, but seller pays freight to destination. Buyer is uninsured during transit unless they arrange their own cover.

name: Carriage and Insurance Paid To
summary: Same as CPT but seller must also arrange insurance to destination.
seller_responsibility: Main carriage costs and insurance to named destination. Export clearance.
buyer_responsibility: Import clearance, duties, unloading at destination.
risk_transfer: When goods are delivered to the first carrier.
insurance: Seller must arrange insurance with minimum cover per ICC Clause A (all-risks). Changed in 2020 — previously only required ICC Clause C (basic).
best_for: When buyer wants assurance that goods are insured during transit and seller pays for it.
watch_out: 2020 change: CIP now requires all-risks insurance (ICC Clause A), a significant upgrade from the basic cover previously required. This makes CIP more expensive than CPT.

name: Delivered at Place
summary: Seller delivers goods to named destination, ready for unloading. Buyer handles import clearance and unloading.
seller_responsibility: All transport and risk to named destination. Export clearance. Goods delivered on arriving vehicle, ready for unloading.
buyer_responsibility: Import clearance, duties, unloading from arriving vehicle.
risk_transfer: At named destination, when goods are ready for unloading from arriving vehicle.
cost_transfer: At named destination (excluding import duties and unloading).
insurance: No obligation, but seller bears risk so has incentive to insure.
best_for: When seller wants to deliver to buyer's door but not handle import formalities in the destination country.
watch_out: Seller bears all transit risk but has no obligation to insure. If goods are damaged in transit, seller bears the loss regardless.

name: Delivered at Place Unloaded
summary: Seller delivers and unloads goods at named destination. Only INCOTERM requiring seller to unload.
seller_responsibility: All transport, risk, and unloading at named destination. Export clearance.
risk_transfer: At named destination, once goods are unloaded.
insurance: No obligation, but seller bears risk.
best_for: When the named destination is a terminal or port where seller can arrange unloading.
watch_out: Renamed from DAT (Delivered at Terminal) in 2020. Now allows delivery at ANY place, not just a terminal. Seller must ensure they can actually unload at the named destination.

name: Delivered Duty Paid
summary: Seller bears all costs and risks to destination including import duties. Maximum obligation for seller.
seller_responsibility: All transport, insurance, export and import clearance, duties, taxes, delivery to named destination.
risk_transfer: At named destination, when goods are ready for unloading.
cost_transfer: At named destination, including all duties and taxes.
insurance: No obligation, but seller bears all risk.
best_for: E-commerce, when seller wants to offer all-inclusive pricing. Maximum convenience for buyer.
watch_out: Seller must be able to handle import clearance in the destination country — may need a local entity or customs broker. VAT/GST implications: seller may need to register for tax in the destination country.

name: Free Alongside Ship
summary: Seller delivers goods alongside the vessel at the port of shipment. Sea and inland waterway only.
seller_responsibility: Deliver goods alongside the vessel at named port. Export clearance.
buyer_responsibility: Loading onto vessel, main carriage, insurance, import clearance, duties.
risk_transfer: When goods are placed alongside the vessel at port of shipment.
cost_transfer: At port of shipment, alongside vessel.
best_for: Bulk cargo or heavy/oversized goods loaded by ship's tackle at port.
watch_out: Sea and inland waterway transport ONLY. Not suitable for containerised cargo (use FCA instead — containers are typically handed to the carrier at a terminal, not alongside the ship).

name: Free on Board
summary: Seller delivers goods on board the vessel at port of shipment. One of the most commonly used terms.
seller_responsibility: Deliver goods on board the vessel at named port. Export clearance. Loading costs.
buyer_responsibility: Main sea freight, insurance, import clearance, duties.
risk_transfer: When goods are on board the vessel at port of shipment.
cost_transfer: At port of shipment, once loaded on board.
best_for: Sea freight where buyer wants to arrange their own shipping and insurance. Very commonly used in international trade.
watch_out: Sea and inland waterway ONLY. Despite being widely used, FOB is technically incorrect for containerised cargo — risk should transfer at the container terminal, not on board the vessel. ICC recommends FCA for containers.

name: Cost and Freight
summary: Seller pays freight to destination port. Risk transfers when goods are loaded on board at port of shipment.
seller_responsibility: Sea freight to named destination port. Export clearance. Loading.
buyer_responsibility: Insurance, import clearance, duties, unloading at destination.
risk_transfer: When goods are on board the vessel at port of shipment.
cost_transfer: At named destination port (freight paid by seller).
best_for: When seller arranges sea freight but buyer handles insurance and destination costs.
watch_out: Like CPT, risk and cost transfer at different points. Goods are at buyer's risk during sea transit even though seller paid the freight. Buyer should arrange marine insurance.

name: Cost, Insurance and Freight
summary: Same as CFR but seller must arrange marine insurance. Risk transfers at port of shipment.
seller_responsibility: Sea freight and insurance to named destination port. Export clearance.
buyer_responsibility: Import clearance, duties, unloading at destination.
risk_transfer: When goods are on board the vessel at port of shipment.
insurance: Seller must arrange insurance — minimum ICC Clause C (basic cover). Note: unlike CIP which requires Clause A, CIF only requires Clause C.
best_for: Sea freight when buyer wants freight and insurance included in the price.
watch_out: CIF only requires basic insurance (ICC Clause C) — buyer may want to arrange additional cover. Not suitable for containerised cargo (use CIP instead). Often confused with CIP but they have different insurance requirements since 2020.
